senses.definition: Occurring behind or after the thoracic (chest) region.
examples: The surgeon noted postthoracic changes on the follow-up scan.
Postthoracic complications can include persistent pain and stiffness.

senses.definition: Located behind or occurring after the thyroid gland.
examples: The scan showed postthyroidal tissue changes.
Postthyroidal complications may require long-term hormone monitoring.

senses.definition: Relating to the area behind the tibia (shin bone).
examples: The athlete reported pain in the posttibial region of the leg.
Posttibial nerve injury can affect foot movement.

senses.definition: Happening immediately after a stressed syllable or sound.
examples: The vowel in the posttonic syllable is often reduced in fast speech.
In this word, the posttonic consonant cluster can be hard for learners.

senses.definition: Relating to the period or condition after exposure to a toxic substance.
examples: The patient required monitoring during the posttoxic phase.
Posttoxic liver damage may appear weeks after the initial exposure.

senses.definition: Located behind the trachea (windpipe).
examples: The mass was found in the posttracheal space.
Posttracheal structures can be hard to see on a standard X-ray.

senses.definition: Occurring after transcription, especially changes to RNA after it is first made from DNA.
examples: Alternative splicing is a key posttranscriptional process that increases protein diversity.
Many diseases are linked to errors in posttranscriptional regulation of RNA.

senses.definition: Happening after a blood transfusion.
examples: The nurse watched for posttransfusion reactions in the first 24 hours.
Posttransfusion monitoring is essential to detect any signs of hemolysis.

senses.definition: Occurring after a protein has been made by a ribosome, especially chemical changes to the protein.
examples: Phosphorylation is a common posttranslational modification of proteins.
Posttranslational changes can turn enzymes on or off.

senses.definition: Located behind the trapezoid bone or muscle.
examples: The posttrapezoid area was tender after the injury.
A small vessel runs through the posttrapezoid region of the wrist.

senses.definition: Happening after, or as a result of, a physical or psychological injury.
examples: She experienced posttraumatic symptoms for years after the accident.
Posttraumatic headaches are common after a mild head injury.

senses.definition: Occurring after a course of treatment has finished, especially medical treatment.
examples: The patient returned for a posttreatment check-up three months later.
Posttreatment side effects can appear weeks after chemotherapy.

senses.definition: After treatment has been given.
examples: The tumor size was measured pre- and posttreatment.
Patients were surveyed six months posttreatment.

senses.definition: Relating to the period after a treaty has been signed.
examples: The posttreaty years were marked by political tension.
Posttreaty negotiations focused on trade and border issues.

senses.definition: Happening after a trial, especially a legal trial.
examples: The defense filed several posttrial motions challenging the verdict.
Victims often feel exhausted during the posttrial phase of a case.

senses.definition: Relating to the period after tuberculosis or to changes caused by past tuberculosis.
examples: The X-ray showed posttubercular scarring in the lungs.
Posttubercular complications can include chronic breathing problems.

senses.definition: Happening immediately after a coughing episode.
examples: Posttussive vomiting is strongly associated with pertussis infection.
The doctor asked whether the child had posttussive wheezing or vomiting.

senses.definition: Located behind the tympanic membrane (eardrum).
examples: Posttympanic cells of the temporal bone were affected by the infection.
The surgeon explored the posttympanic space during the procedure.

senses.definition: Relating to the period after an episode of typhoid fever.
examples: The patient complained of fatigue during the posttyphoid recovery phase.
Posttyphoid complications can affect the intestines and gallbladder.

senses.definition: The state or period of being a postulant, especially in a religious setting.
examples: Her postulance lasted nearly a year before she took the habit.
During his postulance, he shared the daily life of the community.

senses.definition: Plural of postulancy; several periods or states of being a postulant.
examples: The monastery had several postulancies in progress at the same time.
Different orders may structure their postulancies in different ways.

senses.definition: The state of being a postulant or the time spent as a postulant before full entry into a religious order.
examples: Her postulancy gave her time to test her vocation.
The postulancy is usually followed by a longer novitiate.

senses.definition: A person asking to be admitted to a religious order or training for holy orders, usually on a trial basis.
examples: Each postulant spends several months living with the community.
The monastery welcomed three new postulants this year.

senses.definition: Plural of postulant; people who are candidates for entry into a religious order or holy orders.
examples: The abbey houses ten postulants from different countries.
Postulants share in the daily prayers and work of the community.

senses.definition: The position or condition of being a postulant.
examples: Her postulantship in the convent lasted nine months.
He wrote a journal during his postulantship to record his inner journey.

senses.definition: Plural of postulantship; several periods or conditions of being a postulant.
examples: Different communities may structure their postulantships in diverse ways.
Over the years, she guided many postulantships as novice mistress.

senses.definition: Plural of postulatum; things that are assumed to be true without proof, used as a basis for reasoning.
examples: The theory rests on a few simple postulata.
These postulata cannot be proven directly but are widely accepted.

senses.definition: To suggest or claim that something is true as a starting point for reasoning or discussion.
examples: The researchers postulate that the disease has a genetic cause.
Many physicists postulate the existence of parallel universes.

senses.definition: An idea or principle that is accepted as true and used as a basis for reasoning, especially without being proved.
examples: One key postulate of his theory is that space is curved.
The postulates of Euclidean geometry are well known.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of postulate: suggested or claimed something as true for the purpose of reasoning.
examples: He postulated a link between diet and behavior in children.
The existence of dark matter was postulated long before it was indirectly observed.

senses.definition: Third person singular of postulate: suggests or claims something is true as a basis for reasoning.
examples: The model postulates that all particles have a wave nature.
Her theory postulates a direct relationship between stress and illness.

senses.definition: Plural of postulate; ideas or principles accepted as true and used as a basis for reasoning.
examples: The theory is built on three main postulates.
These postulates cannot be tested directly but are widely used.

senses.definition: Present participle of postulate: suggesting or claiming that something is true as a starting point for reasoning.
examples: The authors are postulating a new explanation for the data.
By postulating a common ancestor, they explain the similarities between the species.

senses.definition: The act of suggesting or assuming that something is true, or a thing that is assumed.
examples: His postulation that language shapes thought has been widely debated.
The model is based on the postulation of a constant growth rate.

senses.definition: Relating to or based on postulates or assumptions.
examples: The theory rests on a small set of postulational principles.
His approach is more postulational than empirical.

senses.definition: In a way that depends on or uses postulates or assumptions.
examples: The system is postulationally simple but very powerful.
Postulationally, the model assumes that all agents are rational.

senses.definition: Plural of postulation; several acts of assuming or suggesting that something is true, or several such assumptions.
examples: These postulations form the backbone of his argument.
Many of their postulations have yet to be tested empirically.

senses.definition: In the Roman Catholic Church, a person who officially presents and guides a cause for beatification or canonization.
examples: The postulator gathered documents about the candidate's life.
A Vatican postulator is responsible for promoting the cause of a saint.

senses.definition: Plural of postulator; people who officially guide causes for sainthood in the Catholic Church.
examples: The conference brought together postulators from around the world.
Postulators must follow strict procedures during the investigation.

senses.definition: Having the nature of a postulate; based on an assumption accepted without proof.
examples: This is a purely postulatory statement, not an empirical one.
The system has a postulatory character similar to Euclidean geometry.

senses.definition: A single postulate or assumption taken as true without proof.
examples: From this single postulatum, the rest of the system is derived.
Each postulatum must be clear and free of contradiction.

senses.definition: Located behind or on the back (posterior) side of the ulna bone in the forearm.
examples: Pain in the postulnar region may suggest ligament injury.
The tendon passes through a small postulnar groove.

senses.definition: Located behind or below the umbilicus (navel).
examples: The surgeon made a small postumbilical incision.
A lump in the postumbilical area should be examined carefully.

senses.definition: Located behind the umbo, especially in the shell of a bivalve.
examples: The postumbonal part of the shell has a distinct concave outline.
Growth lines are more visible in the postumbonal area.

senses.definition: Relating to the position and alignment of the body.
examples: Poor postural habits can lead to chronic back pain.
The therapist taught postural exercises to improve alignment.

senses.definition: Relating to changes in body position that affect physical functions.
examples: She suffers from postural hypotension when she stands up quickly.
Postural dizziness can be a sign of low blood pressure.

senses.definition: The way you hold your body when you sit or stand.
examples: Sitting with good posture can reduce neck pain.
The teacher told the students to improve their posture during class.

senses.definition: A particular attitude or way of thinking and behaving about an issue.
examples: The government adopted a tough posture on crime.
His friendly posture helped to calm the angry crowd.

senses.definition: To behave in a way that is intended to impress others or to make a particular impression, often without being sincere.
examples: He likes to posture as a reformer, but he resists real change.
The company is posturing for a better position in the negotiations.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of posture: held the body in a certain way, or behaved in a way meant to impress.
examples: The model postured elegantly for the photographer.
The leader postured as a peacemaker during the summit.

senses.definition: Someone who behaves in a way that is meant to impress others or create a certain image, rather than being sincere.
examples: Critics dismissed him as a mere posturer with no real convictions.
The debate was full of posturers seeking media attention.

senses.definition: Plural of posturer; people who act mainly to impress others or shape their image.
examples: The meeting was dominated by posturers rather than problem-solvers.
On social media, posturers often get more attention than experts.

senses.definition: Plural of posture; different ways of holding the body, or different attitudes to issues.
examples: Yoga includes many different postures to stretch the body.
Countries have taken very different postures on climate change.

senses.definition: Third person singular of posture: holds the body in a certain way, or behaves to create an impression.
examples: He often postures as an independent thinker.
The company postures as environmentally responsible.

senses.definition: Located behind or on the back side of a ureter.
examples: The stone was lodged in a postureteral recess.
A postureteral approach was used to reach the affected area.

senses.definition: Situated behind a ureter.
examples: A postureteric abscess may compress the urinary tract.
The postureteric tissues appeared inflamed on the scan.

senses.definition: Behavior that is mainly intended to impress others or to send a certain message, rather than to be sincere.
examples: The speech was full of political posturing and lacked detail.
Their public disagreement may be more posturing than real conflict.

senses.definition: Present participle of posture: holding the body in a certain way or behaving to impress others.
examples: He stood in the corner posturing for the cameras.
The parties are posturing ahead of the negotiations.
